course_title: Advanced RAG: Retrieval Quality, Evaluation, and Production
description: An intermediate course for developers who have already built a basic RAG pipeline and now want to improve retrieval quality, add robust evaluation, and ship reliably to production. The course covers hybrid search, reranking, query transformation, RAGAS-based evaluation, and the operational disciplines required for production RAG.
summary: Starting from the limitations of a single dense retriever, you will learn how to combine BM25 with embeddings (hybrid search), rerank candidates with cross-encoders and MMR, transform queries with HyDE, Multi-Query, and Decomposition, measure quality with RAGAS, and operate a production RAG system with proper caching, security guardrails, and observability.
target_audience: Developers who have already built a basic RAG MVP using LangChain or a similar framework and want to improve retrieval quality, add evaluation, and harden the system for production. Equivalent experience to completing rag-build-basics or having built a working RAG pipeline independently.

lectures.title: Hybrid Retrieval: Combining Dense Embeddings with Sparse BM25
objectives: Compare the strengths and weaknesses of dense (embedding-based) and sparse (BM25/TF-IDF) retrieval and explain why production RAG almost always combines them
Build a hybrid retriever in LangChain using EnsembleRetriever (Dense + BM25) and tune the weights for your corpus
Implement Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) to merge ranked lists from heterogeneous retrievers without relying on raw scores

lectures.title: Reranking and Context Filtering: Squeezing Precision out of Top-K
objectives: Explain the 2-stage retrieval pattern: a fast bi-encoder for recall followed by a slower but more precise cross-encoder for precision
Wire a reranking model (Cohere Rerank, BGE Reranker, or a local cross-encoder) into a LangChain RAG pipeline using ContextualCompressionRetriever
Use Maximal Marginal Relevance (MMR) to reduce duplicate chunks and balance relevance with diversity in the final context

lectures.title: Query Transformation: HyDE, Multi-Query, and Decomposition
objectives: Explain why a user's raw question is often a poor input to a semantic retriever and how rewriting it improves recall
Implement HyDE (Hypothetical Document Embeddings) to bridge the gap between question-shaped queries and answer-shaped documents
Use LangChain's MultiQueryRetriever to fan a single question into several variants and merge the results
Decompose a complex question into sub-questions, solve each one, and synthesize the final answer

lectures.title: Evaluating RAG: RAGAS and the Four Metrics That Matter
objectives: Define the four core RAG metrics — Faithfulness, Answer Relevancy, Context Precision, Context Recall — and understand exactly what each one diagnoses
Build a small golden set (question + reference answer + expected sources) that can be re-run on every code change
Use the RAGAS framework to compute these metrics with one function call and read the results to decide whether the retriever or the generator is the bottleneck
Track regressions across versions so a small prompt tweak never silently drops accuracy

lectures.title: Production Deployment and Optimization: Cost, Latency, Security, and Observability
objectives: Compare caching strategies (query, embedding, LLM response, semantic cache) and pick the right combination to cut cost and latency
Identify the security threats unique to RAG — prompt injection, PII leakage, data leakage across tenants — and the guardrails that defend against each
Design a monitoring stack that tracks cost, latency, and answer quality together so a regression in one is never hidden by a win in another
Walk through a production-readiness checklist before shipping a RAG application
